raw_consumer_statement:
The merchant submitted forged contract documents to American Express, including a fraudulent cancellation policy page labeled XXXX XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX. This page was not part of my original contract and contains falsified initials. 

American Express denied my dispute based on this forged page, stating I did not follow cancellation policy, even though no cancellation clause exists in the original contract. 

I provided evidence of fraud, falsified documents, and fabricated email correspondence, but AMEX continues to treat this as a cancellation matter and refuses to escalate to fraud review. 

Arbitration was initiated and acknowledged, but AMEXs assigned counsel ( XXXX XXXX ) is non-responsive. No department will escalate or intervene, and AMEX appears to be intentionally delaying until the XX/XX/XXXX deadline so they can close the case and push the {$46000.00} charge despite documented fraud. 

I am requesting CFPB intervention to ensure American Express properly investigates the forged merchant documentation, reclassifies the case under fraud, and halts billing or collection activity pending resolution.
